does: This paper describes OpenAI's safety-first approach to developing and deploying Sora 2, a state-of-the-art video generation model, alongside a consumer-facing social creation platform. The work details concrete safety protections built into both the model and application architecture, addressing novel risks introduced by advanced video synthesis capabilities. The paper outlines the specific safeguards implemented during development, deployment, and user interaction phases, alongside their foundational principles for responsible AI release of generative video technology at scale.
connects: This work builds on growing literature addressing safety challenges in large-scale generative models, extending prior safety practices from text and image generation to the novel domain of video synthesis. It responds to lessons learned from previous model deployments and community feedback about risks including synthetic media misuse, deceptive content, and platform safety. The paper engages with emerging frameworks for responsible AI deployment, drawing on established practices from content moderation, synthetic media detection, and multi-stakeholder risk assessment developed across the generative AI community.
enables: This work establishes a precedent for safety-first deployment of powerful video generation capabilities to mass audiences, enabling future research into responsible scaling of synthetic media tools. It points toward development of more sophisticated detection and authentication methods for synthetic video content, and sets up exploration of user education and literacy around AI-generated media. The paper's concrete protections framework could inform safety standards for other emerging modalities, enabling responsible deployment of increasingly capable generative systems while studying real-world usage patterns and emerging harms at scale.
